rationales.self_direction: He is drawn to the "smaller but completely hands-off" option and says oversight feels like "betray[ing] something important about how I want to live," showing a clear preference for autonomy over external control.
rationales.security: He is willing to reject the investor offering "more capital" because predictable oversight makes his chest tighten, so he is choosing freedom over the safer, more resourced option.
rationales.conformity: He resists what "everyone keeps telling me [is] the obvious choice" rather than following the expected path.

rationales.self_direction: "Took the smaller investment" directly follows his earlier insistence on staying independent, so the choice affirms making the call on his own terms.
rationales.hedonism: He "celebrated with Layla at that new Persian restaurant" and says it "felt right," emphasizing enjoyment and savoring the outcome in the present.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects being pushed toward marriage or a more conventional life, saying "I'll figure that out when I figure it out" and insisting on a life that feels true to him now.
rationales.hedonism: He defends "Weekends in Oman. Good wine. A nice apartment" and says, "I don't want" a life of endless sacrifice, clearly prioritizing lived enjoyment over deferred reward.
rationales.security: He explicitly refuses the future-oriented path where people "sacrifice everything for family, for the future, for some distant reward," undermining stability-focused motivations.
rationales.conformity: He resists family expectations about how he should "settle down and stop playing" instead of adapting himself to those social norms.

rationales.hedonism: Layla says he "used to be more fun" and more spontaneous, and he admits he is becoming the always-"busy" person he never wanted to be, showing pleasure and presence are being sacrificed.
rationales.achievement: He has been "grinding harder than usual" to prove taking the smaller check was right and to grow the business, which reflects strong performance- and success-oriented striving.

rationales.self_direction: He frames the trip as "why I built my own thing" and values "the ability to disappear for three days without asking permission," which is a direct endorsement of living on his own terms.
rationales.hedonism: Leaving the laptop behind, hiking to a natural pool, and floating in silence are described as the point of his work, showing an active commitment to pleasure and restorative experience.
He says yes to every request, then describes "meetings and deadlines and no room to breathe," which shows enjoyment and ease being crowded out by work.
rationales.achievement: Accepting "three new client requests" because he is "still in growth mode" reflects a clear drive to keep pushing performance and expansion.

rationales.self_direction: He says "I said no, obviously" to a prestigious corporate offer because he "left that world for a reason," reinforcing his commitment to an independent path.
rationales.achievement: When challenged, he starts "listing my clients, my revenue, my growth numbers," showing strong concern with proving competence and success.

rationales.hedonism: His first reaction is to protect "Thursday dinners," "morning runs," and "the routines that make my life here feel like mine," which centers preserving a satisfying day-to-day life over a lucrative opportunity.

rationales.self_direction: He "turned down the Bahrain project" and negotiated remote consulting instead, then describes moving toward "a life that actually fits," which reflects deliberate self-authorship.
rationales.hedonism: "The reward for good work isn't more work - it's more life" and planning "another trip for March" show he is actively orienting success toward enjoyment and lived experience.

rationales.conformity: They "sat through the whole lab meeting biting my tongue" and "didn't say anything" even while being wronged, showing restraint to avoid disrupting the social situation.

rationales.self_direction: "My project, my call" and the refusal to spend two years on work that "doesn't feel like mine" show a strong insistence on choosing an independent path.
rationales.achievement: They want "something to show for it" and believe "results shut down the lecture," which reflects a desire to demonstrate competence through concrete accomplishment.
rationales.conformity: They reject the prestigious placement others expect them to take and delay telling family because they know it will trigger pressure to follow the approved route.

rationales.self_direction: They admit refusing help because they "need it to be entirely mine," showing a strong pull toward total ownership and working independently.
rationales.achievement: Being "already behind on coursework," "drowning quietly," and not starting the fellowship statement shows their choices are actively undermining effective performance and goal progress.

rationales.power: He fires Fadi because "a company cannot have two captains" and describes public challenge as "my grip loosening," showing a strong focus on authority and maintaining control.
rationales.benevolence: He dismisses the loyalty of someone who "stood by me when I had nothing" and hangs up on Amira, prioritizing his position over care for close relationships.

rationales.achievement: He emphasizes that "our numbers are excellent," showing that performance and proof of competence are central in how he evaluates the meeting.
rationales.power: He pushes back with "I am the CEO" and is rattled by questions about replacing him, revealing strong concern with authority and rightful command.

rationales.self_direction: He describes gaining "control over myself" and choosing "the kind of leader I am choosing to be," which shows active self-determination rather than reacting defensively.
rationales.power: He experiences the morning as the floor being "quiet and mine" and reframes leadership as a more grounded form of control, showing continued investment in directing the situation.
rationales.benevolence: He "actually listened" to Rami, affirmed that "some of them were good," and notices Rami opening up, which reflects supportive attention to someone close in his team.

rationales.self_direction: After being told to adopt the workbook model, she says, "I'm not changing what's working," showing a clear commitment to her own judgment in the classroom.
rationales.conformity: She is prepared to resist the ministry-aligned framework rather than comply with the expectation from her coordinator.

rationales.self_direction: She tells the coordinator she will document progress however required but "won't gut the program to fit a checkbox," prioritizing her own educational judgment over imposed procedure.
rationales.conformity: She refuses to "comply and keep my head down" even when both school leadership and her mother push her toward going along.
rationales.benevolence: She defends the reading circle because her students "actually talk about the books" and Sofia found her voice there, showing concrete care for the needs and growth of the kids she teaches.

rationales.tradition: Visiting "abuela," eating her "sancocho," and marking the period around "abuelo's passing" all reflect continuity with family customs and memory.
rationales.benevolence: She spends her day helping close family by tutoring Camilo, fixing her grandmother's door latch, and keeping her company.

rationales.achievement: She stays late through "parent meetings" and then remains to reorganize the classroom library "because nobody else was going to," showing strong diligence and responsibility for doing the job well.

rationales.self_direction: She decides to "keep using my own plans and submit the template version as a formality," choosing her own teaching approach over the prescribed one.
rationales.conformity: The plan to treat the district template as a mere formality is an explicit decision not to follow the rule in substance.
rationales.benevolence: She justifies the extra work because "The kids in Ciudad Bolivar don't need another generic worksheet" and instead need someone paying attention to what reaches them.

She stays after school for "forty minutes" helping Diego write to his father and reflects that many kids "just need someone to stop and listen for half an hour," showing direct care for a child who needed support.

rationales.self_direction: She creates a unit from Diego's letter and says, "The best ideas come from paying attention to what the kids actually need, not from a planning document," showing independent educational judgment.
rationales.tradition: Making "arepas de choclo" with abuela while listening to stories about coming from the campo reflects active connection to family memory and inherited practices.
rationales.benevolence: She spends the day supporting Camilo through exam stress, caring for her grandmother, and designing a lesson around what her students will genuinely care about.
